name: Smoked Salmon Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350°F. In a mixer bowl, whip the cheese until very soft. In a food processor, puree the salmon to a paste; add eggs one at a time and the minced shallot. Place the salmon mixture in a bowl; mix in cream, lemon juice, salt, pepper, and sugar; blend well. Fold into whipped cream cheese. Pour into a buttered 7- or 8-inch springform pan. Place the filled pan in a larger baking pan; surround the smaller pan with 1 inch of hot water. Bake for 25 to 30 minutes or until a knife inserted off-center comes out clean. Remove the pan from the water, cool to room temperature, about 1 hour, then chill at least 2 hours.
Meanwhile, make the sauce. In a medium bowl, combine yogurt, sour cream, lemon juice, salt, white pepper, chives, and green onion. Chill for 2 hours. To serve, cut cheesecake into 6 wedges. Divide the sauce among 6 plates, place a wedge on each; garnish with peppers, peppercorns, and fresh herbs.
Per main-dish serving, with yogurt sauce: 330 calories, 27 grams fat, 186 milligrams cholesterol, 585 milligrams sodium.
